title: City: pavement
question: People walk on the ___ next to the road.
explanation: The pavement (or sidewalk) is the hard path beside a road where people walk.

title: City: crossroads
question: Two streets cross each other at a ___.
explanation: A crossroads is the point where two roads cross each other.

title: City: suburb
question: She lives in a quiet ___ outside the city centre.
explanation: A suburb is a residential area on the edge of a large city.

title: City: skyscraper
question: That ___ is over 100 floors tall.
explanation: A skyscraper is a very tall building found in cities, often with many floors.

title: City: pedestrian
question: A ___ is someone who walks in the street.
explanation: A pedestrian is a person walking, especially in an area with traffic.

title: City: traffic jam
question: We were late because of a terrible ___.
explanation: A traffic jam is when vehicles are stuck and moving very slowly or not at all.

title: City: block
question: The shop is two ___s from here.
explanation: A block is the distance along a street between two cross streets in a city.

title: City: alley
question: We took a narrow ___ between the two buildings.
explanation: An alley is a narrow passage between or behind buildings.

title: City: district
question: The financial ___ is in the centre of the city.
explanation: A district is an area of a city or town that has a particular use or character.

title: City: public transport
question: Buses and trains are types of ___.
explanation: Public transport means buses, trains, and other vehicles that anyone can use for a fee.

title: Nature: forest
question: We went for a walk through the ___ and saw many tall trees.
explanation: A forest is a large area covered with trees and other plants growing close together.

title: Nature: river
question: The ___ flows through the valley to the sea.
explanation: A river is a large natural stream of water that flows to the sea or a lake.

title: Nature: mountain
question: The ___ was so high that its peak was hidden in the clouds.
explanation: A mountain is a very high area of land, much higher than a hill.

title: Nature: coast
question: We spent the summer at the ___ and went to the beach every day.
explanation: The coast is the land next to or close to the sea.

title: Nature: valley
question: The village is in a ___ between two mountains.
explanation: A valley is a low area of land between hills or mountains, often with a river.

title: Nature: waterfall
question: We could hear the ___ from a long way away.
explanation: A waterfall is a stream of water that falls from a high place, such as a cliff.

title: Nature: cliff
question: The sea crashes against the ___ far below us.
explanation: A cliff is a high, steep face of rock, often at the edge of the sea.

title: Nature: meadow
question: The cows were eating grass in the ___.
explanation: A meadow is a field of grass, sometimes with wild flowers.

title: Nature: island
question: You can only reach the ___ by boat.
explanation: An island is a piece of land completely surrounded by water.

title: Nature: lake
question: We took a boat out on the ___ surrounded by hills.
explanation: A lake is a large area of fresh water surrounded by land.

title: Descriptions: enormous
question: The new shopping centre is ___.
explanation: Enormous means extremely large. It is much stronger than just 'big'.

title: Descriptions: tiny
question: The baby held my finger with her ___ hand.
explanation: Tiny means extremely small. It is much stronger than just 'small'.

title: Descriptions: ancient
question: They visited an ___ temple that was built 2000 years ago.
explanation: Ancient means very, very old, usually hundreds or thousands of years old.

title: Descriptions: modern
question: The city has many ___ buildings made of glass and steel.
explanation: Modern means relating to the present time or recent times, using new technology or design.

title: Descriptions: steep
question: The road up the hill was very ___.
explanation: Steep means rising or falling at a sharp angle, making it hard to walk up.

title: Descriptions: narrow
question: The street was too ___ for two cars to pass.
explanation: Narrow means having a small distance from one side to the other.

title: Descriptions: deep
question: The swimming pool is two metres ___ at one end.
explanation: Deep means going a long way down from the surface.

title: Descriptions: shallow
question: The water here is only 30 cm deep, so it is very ___.
explanation: Shallow means not deep; having only a short distance from top to bottom.

title: Descriptions: crowded
question: The train was so ___ that I had to stand.
explanation: Crowded means full of people, with very little space.

title: Descriptions: peaceful
question: The countryside was quiet and ___.
explanation: Peaceful means calm and quiet, without noise or worry.

title: Landscape: hill
question: From the top of the ___ we could see the whole town.
explanation: A hill is a raised area of land, smaller than a mountain.

title: Landscape: stream
question: A small ___ ran through the garden.
explanation: A stream is a small, narrow river.

title: Landscape: field
question: The farmer grows wheat in this ___.
explanation: A field is a large open area of land, often used for farming.

title: Landscape: path
question: We followed a ___ through the woods.
explanation: A path is a narrow way for walking, often through the countryside.

title: Landscape: bridge
question: We crossed the ___ over the river.
explanation: A bridge is a structure built over a river, road, or railway so people can cross.

title: Landscape: pond
question: There were ducks swimming in the small ___ in our garden.
explanation: A pond is a small area of still water, smaller than a lake.

title: Landscape: desert
question: It almost never rains in the ___.
explanation: A desert is a large, dry area of land with very little rain and few plants.

title: Landscape: cave
question: The explorers found a ___ in the side of the mountain.
explanation: A cave is a large natural hole in the side of a hill or cliff, or underground.

title: Urban vs Rural: rural
question: They moved to a ___ area to enjoy the quiet life.
explanation: Rural means relating to the countryside, far from cities.

title: Urban vs Rural: urban
question: Traffic is a big problem in ___ areas.
explanation: Urban means relating to a city or town.

title: Urban vs Rural: pollution
question: Cars and factories cause air ___.
explanation: Pollution is damage to air, water, or land caused by harmful substances.

title: Urban vs Rural: commute
question: Her daily ___ to work takes one hour by train.
explanation: A commute is the regular journey between home and work.

title: Urban vs Rural: harvest
question: Farmers collect the ___ in autumn.
explanation: The harvest is the time when crops are collected from the fields.

title: Urban vs Rural: wildlife
question: The nature reserve protects local ___.
explanation: Wildlife means animals and plants that live in natural conditions, not kept by people.

title: City vs Nature: scenery
question: The ___ in the mountains was beautiful.
explanation: Scenery is the natural features of a landscape that you can see, like mountains and trees.

title: City vs Nature: view
question: The hotel room has a wonderful ___ of the lake.
explanation: A view is what you can see from a particular place.

title: Descriptions: vast
question: The Sahara is a ___ desert.
explanation: Vast means extremely large in area or amount.

title: Descriptions: stunning
question: The sunset over the ocean was absolutely ___.
explanation: Stunning means extremely beautiful or impressive.

title: Descriptions: remote
question: The village is very ___; the nearest town is 50 km away.
explanation: Remote means far away from towns and cities, not easy to reach.

title: Landscape: shore
question: We walked along the ___ and collected shells.
explanation: The shore is the land along the edge of the sea or a lake.
